    • Next Steps
  • Guardrails for AI Agents
    • Public API Constraints
      • DO NOT Change Entry-Point Name
      • DO NOT Change Dimension Names
      • DO NOT Add Dependencies Without Approval
    • Data Handling Constraints
      • DO NOT Eagerly Load Full Volumes
      • DO NOT Break Lazy Evaluation Chain
    • Code Quality Constraints
      • DO NOT Suppress Type Errors
      • DO NOT Use Python 3.10+ Exclusive Syntax
    • Testing Constraints
      • DO NOT Require Real VDS Files in Tests
      • DO NOT Commit Large Test Data
    • Documentation Constraints
      • DO NOT Remove Docstrings
      • DO NOT Change Public Function Signatures
    • Architecture Constraints
      • DO NOT Call openvds Directly
      • DO NOT Change Dimension Order
    • Error Handling Constraints
      • DO NOT Silently Swallow Errors
    • Performance Constraints
      • DO NOT Load Metadata Eagerly
      • DO NOT Create Huge Default Chunks
    • Version Control Constraints
      • DO NOT Edit CHANGELOG Directly
      • DO NOT Force-Push to main
    • Known Issues to Avoid
      • Version Mismatch
      • Commented Code
      • Stub Functions
      • Silent Exception Swallowing
    • Summary: The Absolute Don’ts
    • Quick Reference
    • Next Steps
    • When in Doubt

vdsxarray
  • Reference
  • Configuration
  • Edit on GitHub

Configuration

open_dataset Parameters

xr.open_dataset(
    filename_or_obj,
    engine="vds",
    drop_variables=None,
    volume_format="float32",
    name="Amplitude",
    channels=None,
    LOD=0,
    calculate_cdp=False
)

Parameters

filename_or_objstr or Path

Path to the VDS file

drop_variableslist, optional

Variables to drop (not used for VDS)

volume_formatstr, default “float32”

Data format to use

namestr, default “Amplitude”

Name for the data variable

channelslist, optional

Specific channels to read

LODint, default 0

Level of detail

calculate_cdpbool, default False

Whether to calculate CDP coordinates (not yet implemented)

Environment Variables

None currently supported.

Dask Configuration

Configure Dask behavior:

import dask

dask.config.set({
    'array.chunk-size': '128MB',
    'array.slicing.split_large_chunks': True
})
